description: Figure 8 presents a correlative EBSD-ECCI analysis of hydrogen embrittlement micromechanisms in Fe-26Mn-11Al-1.2C austenitic steel. Panel (a) shows an EBSD inverse pole figure (IPF) map with 50 μm scale bar colored by crystallographic orientation relative to the rolling direction (RD). Panel (b) displays the corresponding kernel average misorientation (KAM) map with 0°–5° scale calculated from the first neighboring EBSD point shell at 300 nm spacing, revealing strain localization near the crack path. Panel (c) is an orientation-optimized ECCI micrograph with 10 μm scale bar of the region marked in (b), resolving grain A, grain B, and their boundary. The image captures pronounced planar slip localization intersecting the grain boundary, where chains of microvoids have formed and coalesced. This demonstrates that hydrogen-induced damage in this κ-carbide-containing steel occurs through slip localization-driven intergranular void formation alongside grain boundary cracking.
